1.9.0 (#2927)

* feat: non-ML stay-point visit detection behind Flipper flag (#2832)

* Return 404 instead of 403 for users not in a family

The frontend pings family endpoints by default. When the feature is
unconfigured, repeated 403s look like brute-force attempts and trip
firewall blocks. 404 reflects that the resource simply isn't there
for that user.

* Remove transitional trial-reminder mailers (Manager owns lifecycle now)

Removes the four email types kept temporarily in PR #2508 to let
in-flight Sidekiq jobs drain cleanly. Today (2026-05-17) is the
earliest safe removal date named in those source comments.

Deleted:
- MAILER_REGISTRY entries: trial_expires_soon, trial_expired,
  post_trial_reminder_early, post_trial_reminder_late
- Corresponding mailer methods and the should_skip_email? guard
- Eight email templates (.html.erb / .text.erb)
- All tests and previews specific to these four types
- The four types are removed from user_spec "not enqueued" lists too
  (they're gone; only pure Manager-owned billing types remain)

* Harden 1.7.10 release: PII filtering, area validations, posthog guard

- Add email, name, first_name, last_name, phone to filter_parameters so
  request params attached to error-tracking payloads (PostHog, Sentry)
  and Rails logs are redacted on auth/signup/profile flows.
- Skip PostHog initialization when POSTHOG_API_KEY is blank to avoid
  initializing the client with a nil key.
- Validate Area numericality: radius > 0, latitude in [-90, 90],
  longitude in [-180, 180]. The client-side min on the modal is
  bypassable via direct PATCH /areas/:id.
- Add request specs for PATCH /areas/:id (success, non-positive radius,
  out-of-range coordinates, cross-user 404).
- Document the Cloud-only PostHog telemetry pipeline in CHANGELOG with
  the field redaction list.

* fix: geocode flood (WIP)

* fix: timeline calendar points fallback (WIP)

* test: cover edge cases for timeline calendar points fallback

Adds regression coverage for empty months, points-only bucket clamping,
negative UTC offsets, DST spring-forward, and Lite plan out-of-window points.

* fix: unblock nightly reverse-geocode rescue and bulk-enqueue dedup

NightlyReverseGeocodingJob now passes force: true so points with a lingering
dedup key still get re-enqueued. Jobs::Create#bulk_enqueue claims SETNX keys
on continue_reverse_geocoding and clears them on start_reverse_geocoding,
routing both admin paths through the same dedup. async_reverse_geocode(force:)
deletes the key so a forced run doesn't leave a stale claim.

* fix: namespace geocode dedup key by class and tighten changelog wording

Prefix the Redis dedup key with the class name (geocode:enq:Point:<id>) so
a future Place-side SETNX won't silently collide with Point ids. Update
the CHANGELOG entry to credit the nightly sweep — not the exhausted job
itself — for clearing stale dedup keys.

* fix: release dedup key when reverse-geocode enqueue raises

If perform_later raises after the SETNX claim (e.g. transient Redis blip
between commands), the point was previously locked out for 24 h with no
job behind it. Wrap the non-force enqueue path in a rescue that DELs the
claimed key(s) and re-raises, so a retry can re-claim immediately instead
of waiting for the nightly sweep.

* Drop unused points indexes (not_anomaly, track_generation)

Frees ~7.4 GB on the points table.

- index_points_on_not_anomaly (931 MB): zero lifetime scans;
  planner always picks (user_id, timestamp) and filters anomaly in heap.
- idx_points_track_generation (6.5 GB): used by Track.segment_points_in_sql,
  but the query runs ~64 times lifetime for 0.7s total. Fallback to
  index_points_on_user_id_and_timestamp is acceptable.

index_points_on_raw_data_archive_id stays — it backs FK enforcement on
44M points referencing 1422 archives, even though idx_scan is currently low.
index_points_on_unarchived stays — ARCHIVE_RAW_DATA=true on prod and the
monthly archiver query matches it exactly per EXPLAIN.

* Bulk-insert track segments (Fixes DAWARICH-P3)

Replaces the per-segment .create loop in Tracks::TrackBuilder,
TransportationModes::BackfillJob, and Tracks::Reprocessor with a single
TrackSegment.insert_all via TrackSegments::BulkInserter.

Resolves the N+1 INSERT pattern reported in Sentry DAWARICH-P3 from
Tracks::TimeChunkProcessorJob. Each track now issues one batched INSERT
instead of N round-trips.

- TrackSegments::BulkInserter converts :mode and :confidence symbols to
  enum integers via .fetch (fail-fast on unknown values).
- dominant_mode is computed from segment_data in-memory before insert,
  removing the need to reload created records.
- Returns the input segment_data so callers can inspect what was written.

* Drop per-pair ST_Distance round-trip (Fixes DAWARICH-P1)

Point#distance_to issues a single-pair ST_Distance SELECT per call.
Called inside transportation-mode segmentation loops in
TransportationModes::MovementAnalyzer and SourceDataExtractor, this
adds ~ms of DB latency per consecutive point pair — flagged in Sentry
DAWARICH-P1 as a slow query from Tracks::TimeChunkProcessorJob.

Switch both call sites to the existing in-Ruby Haversine path
(geocoder_distance / Point#distance_to_geocoder). No DB round-trip,
microsecond-scale per pair, same result.

* Drop unused :lonlat from AnomalyFilter point fetch (Fixes DAWARICH-NJ)

Points::AnomalyFilter#fetch_points_with_context selected
id + lonlat + timestamp for every point in a monthly chunk, but Ruby
only ever reads point.id afterwards — lonlat is re-fetched inside the
calculate_all_speeds CTE by id.

For heavy users (>500k points/month) the unused PostGIS geography bytes
dominate transfer cost. Dropping :lonlat from the SELECT reduces row
payload by ~30+ bytes and addresses the slow SELECT flagged in Sentry
DAWARICH-NJ from Points::AnomalyFilterJob.

* Fix Map v2 visits ignoring date range when bbox is sent

Visits::FindWithinBoundingBox now applies start_at/end_at when both
params parse. Since 1.7.10 the Map v2 visits fetch always passes the
viewport bbox, which routed every request through this service. The
service ignored the date range entirely, so the layer rendered all
visits inside the viewport regardless of the selected date filter.

Closes #2817

* Align BackfillJob dominant-mode logic with TrackBuilder

After rebase TrackBuilder uses Track.pick_dominant_mode (distance-weighted,
filters stationary) but BackfillJob still picked the longest-duration
segment. Same input would produce different dominant_mode on the two paths.
Route BackfillJob through the same in-memory-segment + pick_dominant_mode
bridge.

Also: spec coverage for TrackBuilder#update_dominant_mode (empty,
mixed moving+stationary, stationary fallback, nil distance/duration) and a
CHANGELOG entry for the perf work + index drop.

- sidekiq-cron >= 2.4.0 (CVE-2025-67202 admin XSS)

Transitive bumps pulled in by the above:
- aws-partitions, faraday-net_http, fugit, marcel, responders

5791 RSpec examples pass with zero regressions.

* fix(spec): scope place factory to test user in visits PUT specs

The three "updating name together with place_id" specs created
new_place without a user, so the factory assigned a random one.
Since f3dde359 added place_id ownership validation, the controller
correctly returns 422 for a foreign place_id, the visit name is
never updated, and the assertions fail.

Pass user: user to the factory so the specs exercise the
name-preservation logic they were written for.

* feat: non-ML stay-point visit detection behind Flipper flag

Add Visits::StayPointDetector, a deterministic single-pass dwell detector,
gated per-user by the :stay_point_detection Flipper flag (default OFF).
When the flag is off, the existing DBSCAN path is byte-for-byte unchanged
(SmartDetect selects the detector, with a rescue->DBSCAN fallback).

Also adds:
- Visits::ConfidenceScorer: stored 0-100 confidence + breakdown on visits,
  exposed in Api::VisitSerializer (null when flag off).
- PlaceFinder ranking upgrade (exact-name > manual > distance), flag-gated,
  to stop duplicate "Suggested place" rows.
- stay_max_gap_minutes setting; migration adds visits.confidence /
  confidence_breakdown.

No machine learning. Device-evidence (WiFi/charging/velocity) deferred:
production fill rates too low (bssid 6.9%, wifi 7%, charging ~5%).

* fix: families locations history returns 403 (not 404) for non-family users

GET /api/v1/families/locations/history now returns 403 Forbidden when the
requester is not part of a family, matching the documented swagger contract.
The index endpoint keeps its existing 404 (its request + swagger specs assert

Restore the controller to a single ensure_user_in_family! guard returning 404
for both index and history; align the swagger spec/yaml to 404 and drop the
changelog entry. Reverts the 403 change from 5b11e466.

* Fix Trivy image build: npm install before docker build

assets:precompile runs tailwindcss which requires the daisyui node
module; the Dockerfile copies node_modules from the build context, so
deps must be installed first (mirrors build_and_push.yml).

* Fix Trivy install: authenticate GitHub API via GITHUB_TOKEN

The Trivy install script resolves the release tag through the GitHub
API, which is rate-limited (60/hr) when unauthenticated and was failing
the image job. Pass GITHUB_TOKEN so the lookup is authenticated.

* Run Trivy from pinned container; image scan report-only

The trivy-action installer (downloads the binary from GitHub releases)
flaked deterministically in the image job after the heavy docker build.
Switch both Trivy jobs to the pinned ghcr.io/aquasecurity/trivy:0.65.0
container (image scan via docker socket), authenticated to ghcr with
GITHUB_TOKEN. Image scan is report-only on the first pass (SARIF still
uploads); flip --exit-code to 1 to gate on fixable HIGH/CRITICAL.

* feat: Sign in with Apple on the web (cloud only) (#2764)

* feat: Sign in with Apple on the web (cloud only)

Mirrors the existing iOS Apple Sign-in flow for the web app. Cloud-only;
self-hosted instances do not enable the button or routes. Reuses the
existing Auth::VerifyAppleToken and Auth::FindOrCreateOauthUser
services so the email-collision + verified-email guarantees match the
other OAuth providers.

Routes & controller
- GET  /users/auth/apple          → Users::AppleOauthController#request_phase
- POST /users/auth/apple/callback → Users::AppleOauthController#callback
- form_post response_mode (Apple posts the id_token back); CSRF skipped
  only on callback since Apple is the originator
- nonce + state stored in encrypted cookies (httponly, SameSite=None,
  secure) for the callback round-trip; constant-time state compare
- POST callback rate-limited to 20/min per IP (Rack::Attack)

Token verification
- Auth::VerifyAppleToken now accepts an explicit client_id: kwarg —
  iOS continues to verify against APPLE_BUNDLE_ID, web verifies against
  APPLE_WEB_SERVICES_ID

User creation
- New first_name/last_name columns on users; Apple posts user[name]
  exactly once on first authorization, so we capture and persist it
- Omniauthable#omniauth_name_attrs extracts first/last from any
  provider's omniauth payload (uses info.name as fallback) so the same
  pipeline is wired for Google/GitHub when they later provide names
- Auth::FindOrCreateOauthUser accepts name_attrs: and writes them on
  user creation

Feature flag & assets
- New APPLE_WEB_SIGN_IN_ENABLED constant flips on when all four envs
  are set (APPLE_WEB_SERVICES_ID, APPLE_WEB_TEAM_ID, APPLE_WEB_KEY_ID,
  APPLE_WEB_P8_BASE64), and only on cloud
- Sign-in / sign-up forms render the Apple button when enabled
- Empty apple-developer-domain-association.txt placeholder (real file
  will be uploaded out-of-band by ops)

* feat: per-user changelog widget consent with cloud auto-show (#2849)

* feat: per-user changelog widget consent with cloud auto-show

Add an opt-in flow for the chibichange "What's New" widget. On self-hosted
instances each user is prompted once and their choice is recorded in
users.changelog_consent (nullable enum); the widget script is rendered only
when granted, so decliners never contact chibichange. On Cloud, signed-in
users see the changelog with no prompt. Decline/signed-out keep the existing
native GitHub-release badge.

* fix: settings-page changelog opt-in, cloud opt-out, 422 on invalid decision

Add a Settings -> General control to turn What's New notices on or off, so the
navbar prompt's 'change anytime' copy is actually true and Cloud users can opt
out. The indicator helper now honours an explicit decline in every mode. The
consent endpoint returns 422 on an unknown decision instead of a silent 200.
Revert unrelated schema.rb column reorderings, drop the committed plan file,
and add a CHANGELOG entry documenting the feature and the CHIBICHANGE_* vars.

* fix: make changelog_consent migration idempotent

Guard add_column with if_not_exists: true so re-running the migration is safe.

* Filter map Places layer to manual, confirmed, and tagged places (#2861)

* Filter map Places layer to manual, confirmed, and tagged places

Every suggested visit mints a photon Place, so GET /api/v1/places
returned a marker for each one and flooded the map's Places layer
(both v1 and v2 fetch from this endpoint).

Add Place.map_visible(user) — manual OR linked to a confirmed visit
OR tagged — and apply it as the default in PlacesController#index.
A filter param (all/manual/confirmed/tagged) overrides the default
for API consumers that still want the full list.

segment. Same input would produce different dominant_mode on the two paths.
Route BackfillJob through the same in-memory-segment + pick_dominant_mode
bridge.

Also: spec coverage for TrackBuilder#update_dominant_mode (empty,
mixed moving+stationary, stationary fallback, nil distance/duration) and a
CHANGELOG entry for the perf work + index drop.

* Trips update

Migrate trips UI to MapLibre v2: redesigned trip detail page (sticky
map + per-day accordion + timeline replay), MapLibre-based card previews
on /trips, and per-day Notes with their own table and API endpoints.

Per-day stats are computed in a single PostGIS query (ST_MakeLine /
ST_Length on geography) and cache-keyed by both Trip and the points'
max(updated_at).

Antimeridian-aware route rendering: trip preview and per-day route
layer both feed coordinates through RouteSegmenter.unwrapCoordinates,
so transpacific trips no longer draw an impossible line across the
globe (#2731).

Notes are plain text with presence + max-length validation, idempotent
migration (if_not_exists / IF NOT EXISTS), and full Trips::Notes +
Api::V1::Notes coverage.

* fix: assign demo trip notes to renamed description rich text

* fix: keep honest em-dash empty state for trip countries summary

* Reuse Map v2 replay components on trip page, migrate trip form to MapLibre

Replace the forked timeline manager/panel with dev's ReplayManager,
ReplayMarkerLayer, replay CSS, and a shared replay panel partial used by
both Map v2 and the trip page. Rename the trip page's timeline panel and
button to "Replay" with a play icon, use a proper download icon, and
separate time-of-day from distance in the day accordion.

The trip create/edit form now renders its map with MapLibre instead of
Leaflet and live-updates the route preview when the trip dates change;
the Leaflet trips controller is removed.

Also restore the notes table dropped from schema.rb during the rebase
and regenerate swagger docs.

* Fix trip recalc broadcast and harden per-day notes

Re-add the turbo_stream_from subscription dropped during the trip page
redesign so the Recalculate button updates when the job finishes instead
of spinning forever.

Notes:
- rescue RecordNotUnique on concurrent note creation and apply the body
  to the row the other request committed, instead of 500ing
- derive a note's day from its stored UTC value so it lands on the
  correct trip day for users far from UTC, and align the in-app
  uniqueness check with the database index
- drop the unused Point attachable type, which never included Notable

* fix: stop reporting realtime track lock contention to Sentry (#2943)

Tracks::RealtimeGenerationJob now rescues
Tracks::PerUserLock::AcquisitionTimeout separately: it logs a warning and
re-arms the debouncer so the points are retried once the holder releases,
instead of reporting routine per-user lock contention to Sentry.

* fix: match areas by name in place search (#2941)

Searching for a place by name now also matches your areas by name, so an
area outside the nearby radius shows up in the results instead of being
hidden.

* feat: support PUID/PGID for running containers as a non-root user (#2940)

Run the app and Sidekiq containers under a custom user via PUID/PGID:
the container starts as root, fixes ownership of the mounted volumes,
then drops privileges. Use this instead of Compose user:, which cannot
write to root-owned volumes.

* fix: preserve trailing slash in OIDC issuer (#2934)

OIDC login no longer fails with an "Issuer mismatch" error when the
provider's issuer ends in a trailing slash (e.g. Authentik); the trailing
slash is now preserved instead of being stripped.

* fix: align insights and statistics country counts (#2933)

Insights and statistics now report the same number of countries visited,
excluding fly-over countries without a qualifying city.

* fix: skip family member route line when a location update lacks coordinates (#2932)

The Family Members map layer no longer draws a stray line to the map
center when a member's location update is missing coordinates.

* fix: unify suggested-visit confirm UI; confirm visits with no place (#2942)

* fix: allow confirming suggested visits with no candidate places

Suggested visits that have no candidate places now show Confirm and
Delete buttons, so they can be acted on instead of getting stuck.

* refactor: unify suggested-visit confirm UI into one picker

Detection only ever attaches a single place now; the multi-candidate
place_visits suggestions are no longer produced. Collapse the two
suggested-visit branches (multi-place radio picker + the no-suggestions
fallback) into one Confirm/Delete picker, and drop the now-dead
split_suggested_places helper, radio/overflow markup, and CSS.

* docs: reword suggested-visit changelog entry for unified confirm UI

* fix: prevent course_accuracy overflow from dropping point batches (#2947)

A near-stationary iOS fix can report a course_accuracy far above the
numeric(8,5) ceiling (e.g. 1731.7). Since Points::Create inserts a whole
batch via a single upsert_all, one such value raised
ActiveRecord::RangeError and discarded every point in the request,
returning 500 to the client.

Sanitize course and course_accuracy in Points::Params: values that are
non-numeric, non-finite, or whose absolute value reaches the column
limit are stored as nil, so the rest of the batch persists.

* Add instapods badge

* fix: declare changelog_consent attribute type so migrations load User (#2959)

The DedupeTracksForUniqueIndex data migration (20260508193900) loads the
User model before AddChangelogConsentToUsers (20260602125716) adds the
backing column. On a 1.7.x -> 1.8.1 upgrade both migrations are pending and
run oldest-first, so the dedupe job hits User.find_by while the column is
still absent. ActiveRecord 8.0 then raises:

  Undeclared attribute type for enum 'changelog_consent' in User.

Declaring an explicit attribute type keeps the enum valid even when the
column has not been added yet; once it exists the declaration is a no-op.

* feat: AirTrail flight integration (#2831)

* feat: pull AirTrail flights into Dawarich and show them on the map

One-way AirTrail integration: Dawarich pulls a user's flight history
from their AirTrail instance via its REST API, stores flights as a
first-class Flight model, and renders them as arcs on the MapLibre (v2)
map. When the Flights layer is enabled, overlapping GPS points/routes/
tracks are hidden so AirTrail data has render priority.

Backend: Flight model + migration, AirTrail::Client / ConnectionTester /
FlightMapper / ImportFlights, ImportFlightsJob + daily SyncSchedulingJob,
integrations settings card with Sync now, Api::V1::FlightsController
(GeoJSON) + serializer.

Frontend: FlightsLayer, flight_mask util, data-loader / layer-manager /
routes-manager wiring, and a Flights toggle in the map settings panel.

* fix: harden AirTrail integration after review

- restore GPS layers when the flights mask has no time windows
- render the Flights toggle only for users with AirTrail configured
- escape flight properties in the map popup
- survive concurrent sync upserts (per-flight savepoint + RecordNotUnique rescue)
- write airtrail_last_synced_at via jsonb_set instead of replacing the settings hash
- pre-filter the daily scheduling job to configured users
- fall back to default range on unparseable flights API date params
- CHANGELOG entry

* feat: draw flights as great-circle arcs

Interpolate each flight's 2-point LineString into a great-circle path
client-side (16-128 points by angular distance), with longitude
unwrapping across the antimeridian. The flights API payload is
unchanged.

* fix: harden AirTrail flights from second review pass

- mask GPS tracks within flight windows using their ISO start_at/end_at
  (track masking was a silent no-op before)
- Flight.mappable scope: flights API excludes flights missing airport coords
- cap the flights API at MAX_FLIGHTS and order by departure_time
- ImportFlightsJob rescues AirTrail::Client::Error -> notification + reporter,
  then re-raises for Sidekiq retry semantics

* style: render flight arcs in the tracks line style

Use the track indigo (#6366F1), 4px solid, 0.7 opacity instead of the
dashed amber, so flights match the rest of the map's line styling.

* fix: unify Google OAuth identity across web/mobile + connected-provider indicator (#2969)

* fix: write google_oauth2 provider on mobile Google sign-in

The mobile Google endpoint stored provider='google' while the web
OmniAuth flow uses 'google_oauth2'. Since identity is resolved by
(provider, uid), the same Google account signing in on web after
registering on mobile missed the identity lookup and fell through to
the email-collision path, prompting the user to "link" an account they
already own.

Normalize the mobile provider to the OmniAuth-canonical 'google_oauth2'
so a Google account resolves to a single user across platforms. Mobile
auth is unreleased, so no existing rows need backfilling.

* fix: show connected-provider indicator on account settings

The shared devise links partial rendered the OAuth sign-in buttons with
no signed_in? guard, so an OAuth user saw a "Sign in with Google" button
on their own account settings page with no sign that the account was
already linked.

Guard the omniauth buttons block with !signed_in? (covering Google,
Apple, GitHub and OIDC), and render a "Connected with {provider}"
indicator on the settings page for oauth users.

* refactor: simplify oauth_provider_display_name and add coverage

Delegate GitHub, OIDC and unknown providers to the existing
oauth_provider_name helper instead of re-implementing the mapping, and
drop the dead defined?(OIDC_PROVIDER_NAME) guard (the constant is always
set). Add characterization specs for oauth_provider_display_name and a
CHANGELOG entry for the Google identity + connected-provider fixes.

* refactor: drop dead legacy 'google' provider branch

The mobile endpoint now writes 'google_oauth2', so a 'google' provider
string is never stored; the else branch already maps it to "Google" via
oauth_provider_name, so the explicit case is redundant.

* Update sampling rate

* fix: remove stray singleton_method_added call from Sentry initializer

* feat: surface stay_max_gap_minutes in map settings panel (#2972)

* feat: surface stay_max_gap_minutes in map settings panel

Adds a 'Visit Max Gap' slider to the Map v2 settings panel so users can
tune the stay-point detector's max gap (minutes) between consecutive
points within a single visit. The setting already existed in
SafeSettings (default 60, clamped 5-720) and StayPointDetector but was
not editable from the UI or permitted by the settings API.

- Permit :stay_max_gap_minutes in Api::V1::SettingsController
- Add slider + target/action wiring in the MapLibre settings panel
- Map camelCase<->snake_case and load/save coercion in SettingsManager

* fix: gate Visit Max Gap slider behind stay_point_detection flag

The stay_max_gap_minutes setting only affects StayPointDetector, which
runs only when the :stay_point_detection Flipper flag is enabled
(default off). Render the slider only when the flag is on so it isn't
dead UI for users without the feature.

- Wrap the Visit Detection block in a Flipper.enabled? guard
- Add CHANGELOG entry
- Add request specs asserting read-time clamp (1000->720, 1->5)

* fix: don't write stay_max_gap_minutes when its gated input is absent

When :stay_point_detection is off the Visit Max Gap slider isn't
rendered, so the advanced-settings form has no stayMaxGapMinutes field.
The collect step ran parseInt(null) -> NaN, which serialized to 'NaN'
and clamped to 5 on read, silently clobbering the saved value on any
Apply Settings click. Only collect the key when the input is present.

- Guard the collect with formData.has, matching the transportation
  thresholds conditional pattern
- Add a request spec asserting an omitting patch preserves the value

* docs: name the stay_point_detection flag in the changelog entry

Make explicit that the Visit Max Gap control is gated behind the
stay_point_detection feature flag, which is off by default.

* feat: public location sharing (#2804)

* feat: public location sharing (trips + timeline)

* feat: public per-track and live-location sharing

Add two public-share types on top of the existing SharedLink infra:

- Per-track sharing: a Share button on each track card creates an
  expiring public link with the track route, stats and optional photos
  (Tracks::ShareLinksController, _track viewer, :track cases in the
  shared points/photos APIs).
- Live-location sharing: share current position in real time from the
  Map v2 Tools tab. A token-gated anonymous ActionCable connection lets
  public viewers subscribe to SharedLocationChannel for one active live
  share only (phrase-gated via the encrypted unlock cookie); all other
  channels reject anonymous connections. Points::LiveBroadcaster pushes
  the privacy-masked latest point; revoke/regenerate tear down open
  viewers.

Harden shared/links dispatch against unknown resource types. No
migration (columns already existed).

* feat: public share trip redesign, unified replay, CTA + polish

- Rework the public trip share page to mirror the in-app trip layout
  (two-column sticky map, day-by-day accordion, day hover/click → map
  highlight) with per-section visibility toggles in the trip share form.
- Extract replay into a single reusable ReplayPanel and migrate the
  Map v2 controller, the trip page, and the public share onto it
  (proportional segment timing, easing follow that yields to user pans).
- Self-hosted instances no longer rate-limit public shared pages.
- Public-page CTA: dark band + header button, UTM-tagged via a
  public_share_cta_url helper, cloud-oriented copy.
- Shared layout: logo in the header, favicon always light, prominent CTA.
- TripDays day-stats service; harden share-link dispatch.

No migration (columns already existed).

* feat: unified share hub + live-share enhancements

- Share hub modal on Map v2 (Live location + Time period tabs, conditional
  Shared list); live-share indicator dot on the search-bar Share button,
  updated instantly via Turbo Stream on create/revoke
- Live shared page: smooth marker interpolation, time label, "last seen …
  ago" ticker, route polyline behind a "share the route" option, track-color
  line, unified recent-point marker
- Generic revoke-by-id endpoint; ShareLinks::HubData query object;
  HubStreamable concern; hub-aware Managable responses
- Order shared track/trip points by timestamp (fix spiderweb polyline)
- Search-bar: container-aligned, hosts the share trigger; move modal mount to
  layout body so the backdrop covers the navbar uniformly
- Add external-link icon; modal polish (constant size, tooltips, no blink)

* chore: drop ferrum/Chrome OG rendering; static OG image + share cleanups

- Remove ferrum gem and the dynamic OG-image subsystem (renderer, job,
  controller, access guard, og.html view, og.png/og.html routes,
  og_image_state column/enum, og_image attachment); og:image now points to
  the static og_default.png (refreshed to the new logo)
- create_shared_links migration: drop pgcrypto (gen_random_uuid is core on
  PG 13+), drop og_image_state, make idempotent (if_not_exists)
- Revert en.yml; inline the invalid-phrase flash string
- Extract _trip.html.erb business logic into SharedLinks::TripPresenter

* fix: privacy-zone masking for shared photos + share endpoint hardening

- Mask photos inside privacy zones (drop from index + thumbnail allow-list),
  matching the points masking; privacy_zones moved to the shared base controller
- Cache non-protected shared points/route/photos responses (short public TTL);
  skip caching for phrase-locked shares and the real-time live marker
- Broadcast {revoked:true} to old live shares before auto-revoke on recreate
- Phrase-unlock cookie TTL follows the link expiry instead of a fixed hour
- Drop leftover chromium + FERRUM_BROWSER_PATH from the Docker image

* fix: mask privacy zones in trip day-stats + harden photo thumbnail cache

- TripDays day-stats now exclude privacy-zone points (first/last time +
  distance) via the same ST_DWithin filter as the shared route, so the day
  accordion no longer leaks dwell metadata for masked segments
- Include a privacy-zone fingerprint in the thumbnail allow-list cache key so
  adding a zone busts stale masked photo ids instead of serving them

* Harden shared-link security: CSPRNG phrase gen, cable throttle, unlock logging

* ci(trivy): ignore AVD-DS-0002 (image runs as root; drops privs via gosu)

* test: fix stale specs for 1.9.0 (airtrail settings, share hub entry, sidekiq schedule isolation)

* style: fix rubocop offenses in 1.9.0 changes (redundant freeze, heredoc, word array, line length)

* feat: verify supporters by GitHub username (#2980)

GitHub Sponsors with a private email are not included in the verify-service
webhook payload as an email, only as a login. Add a second verification path
that resolves supporter status by GitHub username so those sponsors can earn
the supporter badge.

- Supporter::VerifyGithubUsername queries the verify service with
  ?github_username= and caches like the email path
- User#supporter_info checks email first, then GitHub username
- safe_settings exposes supporter_github_username
- settings form gains a GitHub username field; verify_supporter accepts either
  an email or a username

* fix: compute trip day stats in the user's IANA timezone

Per-day trip stats on the trip page and public shared trip pages bucketed
points with a doubled `AT TIME ZONE 'UTC' AT TIME ZONE tz` expression on
`to_timestamp(...)`, which shifted points near local midnight into the
adjacent day. The controller also fed the raw `current_user.timezone`
(an ActiveSupport friendly name) into the SQL, raising
PG::InvalidParameterValue and 500'ing the trip page for any non-IANA
timezone.

Use `current_user.timezone_iana` and a single `AT TIME ZONE`, matching
the rest of the stats queries.

* fix: preserve a saved supporter credential when verifying the other one

verify_supporter overwrote both supporter_email and
supporter_github_username with the submitted params, so re-verifying with
one field cleared wiped the other previously-saved credential. Only write
each field when its submitted value is present.

Also records the GitHub-username supporter verification in the 1.9.0
CHANGELOG (#2980).

* Return 404 instead of 403 for users not in a family

The frontend pings family endpoints by default. When the feature is
unconfigured, repeated 403s look like brute-force attempts and trip
firewall blocks. 404 reflects that the resource simply isn't there
for that user.

* Remove transitional trial-reminder mailers (Manager owns lifecycle now)

Removes the four email types kept temporarily in PR #2508 to let
in-flight Sidekiq jobs drain cleanly. Today (2026-05-17) is the
earliest safe removal date named in those source comments.

Deleted:
- MAILER_REGISTRY entries: trial_expires_soon, trial_expired,
  post_trial_reminder_early, post_trial_reminder_late
- Corresponding mailer methods and the should_skip_email? guard

- Add email, name, first_name, last_name, phone to filter_parameters so
  request params attached to error-tracking payloads (PostHog, Sentry)
  and Rails logs are redacted on auth/signup/profile flows.
- Skip PostHog initialization when POSTHOG_API_KEY is blank to avoid
  initializing the client with a nil key.
- Validate Area numericality: radius > 0, latitude in [-90, 90],
  longitude in [-180, 180]. The client-side min on the modal is
  bypassable via direct PATCH /areas/:id.
- Add request specs for PATCH /areas/:id (success, non-positive radius,

  the field redaction list.

Adds regression coverage for empty months, points-only bucket clamping,
negative UTC offsets, DST spring-forward, and Lite plan out-of-window points.

* fix: unblock nightly reverse-geocode rescue and bulk-enqueue dedup

NightlyReverseGeocodingJob now passes force: true so points with a lingering
dedup key still get re-enqueued. Jobs::Create#bulk_enqueue claims SETNX keys
on continue_reverse_geocoding and clears them on start_reverse_geocoding,
routing both admin paths through the same dedup. async_reverse_geocode(force:)
deletes the key so a forced run doesn't leave a stale claim.

* fix: namespace geocode dedup key by class and tighten changelog wording

Prefix the Redis dedup key with the class name (geocode:enq:Point:<id>) so
a future Place-side SETNX won't silently collide with Point ids. Update
the CHANGELOG entry to credit the nightly sweep — not the exhausted job
itself — for clearing stale dedup keys.

* fix: release dedup key when reverse-geocode enqueue raises

If perform_later raises after the SETNX claim (e.g. transient Redis blip
between commands), the point was previously locked out for 24 h with no
job behind it. Wrap the non-force enqueue path in a rescue that DELs the
claimed key(s) and re-raises, so a retry can re-claim immediately instead
of waiting for the nightly sweep.

* Drop unused points indexes (not_anomaly, track_generation)

Frees ~7.4 GB on the points table.

- index_points_on_not_anomaly (931 MB): zero lifetime scans;
  planner always picks (user_id, timestamp) and filters anomaly in heap.
- idx_points_track_generation (6.5 GB): used by Track.segment_points_in_sql,
  but the query runs ~64 times lifetime for 0.7s total. Fallback to
  index_points_on_user_id_and_timestamp is acceptable.

index_points_on_raw_data_archive_id stays — it backs FK enforcement on
44M points referencing 1422 archives, even though idx_scan is currently low.
index_points_on_unarchived stays — ARCHIVE_RAW_DATA=true on prod and the
monthly archiver query matches it exactly per EXPLAIN.

* Bulk-insert track segments (Fixes DAWARICH-P3)

Replaces the per-segment .create loop in Tracks::TrackBuilder,
TransportationModes::BackfillJob, and Tracks::Reprocessor with a single
TrackSegment.insert_all via TrackSegments::BulkInserter.

Resolves the N+1 INSERT pattern reported in Sentry DAWARICH-P3 from
Tracks::TimeChunkProcessorJob. Each track now issues one batched INSERT
instead of N round-trips.

- TrackSegments::BulkInserter converts :mode and :confidence symbols to
  enum integers via .fetch (fail-fast on unknown values).
- dominant_mode is computed from segment_data in-memory before insert,
  removing the need to reload created records.
- Returns the input segment_data so callers can inspect what was written.

* Drop per-pair ST_Distance round-trip (Fixes DAWARICH-P1)

Point#distance_to issues a single-pair ST_Distance SELECT per call.
Called inside transportation-mode segmentation loops in
TransportationModes::MovementAnalyzer and SourceDataExtractor, this
adds ~ms of DB latency per consecutive point pair — flagged in Sentry
DAWARICH-P1 as a slow query from Tracks::TimeChunkProcessorJob.

Switch both call sites to the existing in-Ruby Haversine path
(geocoder_distance / Point#distance_to_geocoder). No DB round-trip,
microsecond-scale per pair, same result.

* Drop unused :lonlat from AnomalyFilter point fetch (Fixes DAWARICH-NJ)

Points::AnomalyFilter#fetch_points_with_context selected
id + lonlat + timestamp for every point in a monthly chunk, but Ruby
only ever reads point.id afterwards — lonlat is re-fetched inside the
calculate_all_speeds CTE by id.

For heavy users (>500k points/month) the unused PostGIS geography bytes
dominate transfer cost. Dropping :lonlat from the SELECT reduces row
payload by ~30+ bytes and addresses the slow SELECT flagged in Sentry
DAWARICH-NJ from Points::AnomalyFilterJob.

* Fix Map v2 visits ignoring date range when bbox is sent

Visits::FindWithinBoundingBox now applies start_at/end_at when both
params parse. Since 1.7.10 the Map v2 visits fetch always passes the
viewport bbox, which routed every request through this service. The
service ignored the date range entirely, so the layer rendered all
visits inside the viewport regardless of the selected date filter.

Closes #2817

* Align BackfillJob dominant-mode logic with TrackBuilder

After rebase TrackBuilder uses Track.pick_dominant_mode (distance-weighted,
filters stationary) but BackfillJob still picked the longest-duration
segment. Same input would produce different dominant_mode on the two paths.
Route BackfillJob through the same in-memory-segment + pick_dominant_mode
bridge.

Also: spec coverage for TrackBuilder#update_dominant_mode (empty,
mixed moving+stationary, stationary fallback, nil distance/duration) and a
CHANGELOG entry for the perf work + index drop.

* fix(spec): scope place factory to test user in visits PUT specs

The three "updating name together with place_id" specs created
new_place without a user, so the factory assigned a random one.
Since f3dde359 added place_id ownership validation, the controller

* feat: non-ML stay-point visit detection behind Flipper flag

Add Visits::StayPointDetector, a deterministic single-pass dwell detector,
gated per-user by the :stay_point_detection Flipper flag (default OFF).
When the flag is off, the existing DBSCAN path is byte-for-byte unchanged
(SmartDetect selects the detector, with a rescue->DBSCAN fallback).

Also adds:

  confidence_breakdown.

production fill rates too low (bssid 6.9%, wifi 7%, charging ~5%).

* feat: Sign in with Apple on the web (cloud only) (#2764)

* feat: Sign in with Apple on the web (cloud only)

Mirrors the existing iOS Apple Sign-in flow for the web app. Cloud-only;
self-hosted instances do not enable the button or routes. Reuses the
existing Auth::VerifyAppleToken and Auth::FindOrCreateOauthUser
services so the email-collision + verified-email guarantees match the
other OAuth providers.

Routes & controller
- GET  /users/auth/apple          → Users::AppleOauthController#request_phase
- POST /users/auth/apple/callback → Users::AppleOauthController#callback
- form_post response_mode (Apple posts the id_token back); CSRF skipped
  only on callback since Apple is the originator
- nonce + state stored in encrypted cookies (httponly, SameSite=None,
  secure) for the callback round-trip; constant-time state compare
- POST callback rate-limited to 20/min per IP (Rack::Attack)

Token verification
- Auth::VerifyAppleToken now accepts an explicit client_id: kwarg —
  iOS continues to verify against APPLE_BUNDLE_ID, web verifies against
  APPLE_WEB_SERVICES_ID

User creation
- New first_name/last_name columns on users; Apple posts user[name]
  exactly once on first authorization, so we capture and persist it
- Omniauthable#omniauth_name_attrs extracts first/last from any
  provider's omniauth payload (uses info.name as fallback) so the same
  pipeline is wired for Google/GitHub when they later provide names
- Auth::FindOrCreateOauthUser accepts name_attrs: and writes them on
  user creation

Feature flag & assets
- New APPLE_WEB_SIGN_IN_ENABLED constant flips on when all four envs
  are set (APPLE_WEB_SERVICES_ID, APPLE_WEB_TEAM_ID, APPLE_WEB_KEY_ID,
  APPLE_WEB_P8_BASE64), and only on cloud
- Sign-in / sign-up forms render the Apple button when enabled
- Empty apple-developer-domain-association.txt placeholder (real file
  will be uploaded out-of-band by ops)

refactor(docker): Consolidate Docker Compose setup to use profiles

Consolidate multiple Docker Compose configuration files into a single
`docker-compose.yaml` file. This simplifies the overall Docker Compose setup by
leveraging profiles to manage different environments (e.g., dev-postgres,
dev-sqlite) and application modes (app, dev). It reduces file redundancy and
improves the maintainability of the Docker configuration.

Update Makefile commands to utilize the new Docker Compose profiles. The
`dev-docker-postgres`, `dev-docker-sqlite`, and `dev-docker-clean` targets now
correctly invoke Docker Compose with the appropriate profiles and project
directory, ensuring compatibility with the consolidated setup.

Remove the `sqlite.env` file as its configuration is now inlined within the
`docker-compose.yaml`. This further streamlines the setup by directly specifying
the SQLite database driver and DSN in the `dev-workout-tracker-sqlite` service
definition, eliminating the need for a separate environment file.

fix(scanner): make enumeration visible, bounded, and cancellable (#303)

A scan stuck in the directory-walk phase was indistinguishable from a
hang: the only log line was 'Starting scan', no scan row existed in
the DB yet (so /scans and the dashboard showed nothing), the walk
ignored cancellation (pressing Cancel did nothing until enumeration
finished), and there was no timeout so a hung mount hung the scan
forever. Diagnosed live on a mergerfs-over-CIFS mount where degraded
network storage made each stat block for seconds.

Four changes:

1. Visibility: the scan row is now created up front in a new
   'enumerating' state (migration 011 extends the scans.status CHECK
   to allow 'enumerating' and 'scanning' — values the code already
   referenced but the constraint silently forbade). The row flips to
   'scanning' once the walk completes (FinishEnumeration persists the
   file list + total). /scans and the dashboard show the scan
   immediately.

2. Progress: enumerateMediaFiles emits a throttled 'Enumerating PATH:
   N media files found so far (M entries scanned)' heartbeat every 5s,
   so a slow mount is visibly working instead of looking hung.

3. Cancellation: enumerateMediaFiles takes a context and checks
   ctx.Err() on every entry, so a user cancel (which cancels the scan
   ctx) aborts the walk promptly. The row is marked 'cancelled'.

4. Timeout: the walk runs under HEALARR_SCANNER_ENUMERATION_TIMEOUT
   (default 30m). On timeout the scan is marked 'aborted' with an
   explanatory error_message pointing at slow/unresponsive storage,
   instead of hanging until a container restart.

Reconcile interplay: an orphaned 'enumerating' row (process died
mid-walk) has current_file_index=0, so ReconcileOrphans marks it
'cancelled' (not resumable) — correct, there's no file list to
resume from.

Tests: repo CreateEnumerating/FinishEnumeration + enumerating-orphan
reconcile; scanner timeout env parsing, walk-honors-cancellation, and
handleEnumerationFailure status mapping (cancel->cancelled,
timeout->aborted, error->aborted). Full services suite green under
-race.

fix: quote SESSION_SECRET in docker-compose files to prevent YAML parsing issues

Random key generators often produce keys with special characters like #, $, !
which YAML interprets as comments or special syntax. This causes the value to
be truncated, leading to 'must contain at least 32 characters' errors even
when users paste 32+ character keys.

Changes:
- Quote SESSION_SECRET placeholder in all docker-compose files
- Add warning comment about keeping quotes for special characters
- Update Windows Docker Desktop docs with troubleshooting for this issue
- Update example configurations to show quoted values

Affected files:
- docker-compose.windows.yml
- docker-compose.prod.yml
- docker-compose.synology.yml
- docker-compose.qnap.yml
- docker-compose.unraid.yml
- docs/admin/windows-docker-desktop.md
